story_premise: A classic adaptation with source-text compression, copyright review, three foreshadows, two payoffs, and a final twist.

story_graph.protagonist_want: understand why the old house repeats one dinner
story_graph.reader_care_reason: the protagonist must choose whether to open the sealed letter

nodes.typed_hook: The old dinner table is set for one guest who has not arrived in fifty years.
nodes.typed_narration: The protagonist hides the sealed letter in a sleeve before the host notices the empty chair.
nodes.next_page_question: Why does the host still serve the absent guest?
nodes.final_hook_sentence: The empty chair scrapes backward by itself.
nodes.reader_retention_goal: Compress the classic premise into one visible mystery.
nodes.visual_memory_point: an empty chair moving beside a sealed letter
nodes.protagonist_want: learn who the missing guest is
nodes.reader_care_reason: the protagonist has the only unread letter
nodes.concrete_behavior: the protagonist folds the letter into a sleeve

nodes.typed_hook: The letter names the guest as the person reading it.
nodes.typed_narration: The protagonist opens the letter and sees the same table drawn with one extra empty chair.
nodes.final_hook_sentence: The chair at the beginning scrapes back into place.
nodes.reader_retention_goal: Pay off the opening chair and letter.
nodes.visual_memory_point: a drawn table adding one extra chair
nodes.opening_callback: The moving empty chair from the opening returns.
nodes.twist_reveal: The missing guest is the next reader of the letter.
nodes.reader_care_reason: opening the letter changes the protagonist's fate
nodes.concrete_behavior: the protagonist steps away from the chair
